title: The Supper Prepared
subtitle: At the first vespers of the Last Supper, two disciples go to make ready the upper room

stillness: The hour of mercy is at hand.
Be still, and make yourself present.
A jar of water of tears must go before the supper.
gospel: On the first day of unleavened bread,
when the paschal lamb was to be slain,
the Lord sent Peter and John, saying:
a man will meet you carrying a jar of water.
Follow him into the house he enters,
and say to the master of the house:
The Master says: My time is near;
with you I keep the Passover with my disciples.
And the disciples went, and found it as he had told them,
and they made ready the Passover.
behold: Now the time of the Lord's pity and compassion draws near,
the time he had appointed to make his people safe,
to redeem them not with corruptible gold or silver,
but with his own precious blood.
Behold him: he willed to keep a notable supper with his disciples
before he should depart from them by death,
as a sign and memorial of his remembrance.
Gaze upon these things, present, with the highest attention;
for if you do this worthily and watchfully,
the kind Lord will not suffer you to go away fasting.
Behold the upper room, great and furnished, on Mount Sion,
and behold the man carrying the jar of water:
where Christ is to sup, the jar of water of tears,
the water of cleansing, must go before in expiation.
consider: Behold here the deepest poverty of Christ.
So poor was he that he had neither house nor lodging,
nor anything with which to buy the paschal food;
and therefore the disciples asked him where they should prepare the Passover.
Let those hear this and blush
whose care is to build great and ambitious houses,
Let them acknowledge Christ, the Lord of all,
who had no place to sup, nor even to lay his head,
nor food to take, nor money to spend.
Let them see how little they stand apart from the unbelievers,
who worship idols and the works of men's hands.
For to set one's zeal and delight in great and beautiful buildings, or in treasures —
what else is it than to worship wood, or stones, or silver and gold, with the heathen?
The water is the laver of grace;
the jar is the frailty of those through whom that grace was to be given to the world.
prayer: who at the hour of vespers kept the last supper with your disciples
in the great upper room, furnished,
and fed them with your most sacred Body and Blood:
make my breast an upper room, great and furnished;
enlarge hope and charity in my heart;
magnify in me long-suffering, hope and charity,
Grant that my heart, contrite and humbled,
may receive you in its small measure —
you whom heaven and earth cannot contain;
I may think and accomplish all things pleasing to you,
and turn away from all things hateful and contrary;
and so, persevering to the end,
I may then attain a worthy receiving
of your most holy Body and Blood.

latinJewel.latin: Et significat quod ubi Christus cœnaturis est, lagena aquæ lacrymarum in expiatione debet præcedere.
latinJewel.english: And this signifies that where Christ is to sup, the jar of the water of tears must go before, in expiation.
